title: Add xlsxwriter to dev dependencies
description: tests/test_chat_history_router.py excel tests fail with ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'xlsxwriter'. Pre-existing, unrelated to UniProt, but it breaks every quality gate (2 failed, 568 passed on full suite as of f3b4ce0).

title: Verify UniProt epic end to end against a live agent
description: The UniProt epic (genetics-mcp-server-fmq) shipped in f3b4ce0 but its ACTUAL goal is unverified: that a model wired to these tools stops asserting accessions from memory. Nothing has been built or deployed - newest image is tagged bef5e46, the commit before the work. Required: build+deploy, then run the question that originally failed ('what are the active site residues of PRSS55?') and check the agent reaches Q6UWB4 unaided rather than Q7Z5A4/Q9H0E5. Also exercise map_protein_variants, which has had no end-to-end run at all. Do NOT consider the epic done until this passes.
notes:
Partial: live chat session confirmed the epic's core goal — asked for PRSS55 active-site residues, the agent reached Q6UWB4 unaided (original failure asserted Q7Z5A4/Q9H0E5). Still open here: build+deploy of the image (was at bef5e46), and map_protein_variants has not been exercised live.
Progress 2026-07-23 — everything short of the production deploy is done:

DONE:
- Live-agent goal met (user-confirmed): PRSS55 active-site query reached Q6UWB4 unaided.
- map_protein_variants exercised against live APIs: A70P on TPO/P07202 -> agrees=false with the Sequence conflict feature attached; P70L -> agrees=true. Coordinates chr2:1,433,466-1,433,468 collapsed from 7 transcript rows.
- Deploy artifact validated: 'docker build' succeeds on the current tree; the image runs and imports cleanly (uniprot module + pure fns + the d1k _refused_origin pin + the 3 registered tool methods + fastexcel runtime present; xlsxwriter correctly absent as dev-only; chat_api app = 47 routes).

REMAINING (ops, needs credentials — not done autonomously):
- Build+push image to Artifact Registry and 'kubectl' rollout via genetics-results-suite/scripts (build.sh, deploy.sh/rollout.sh; k8s/deployments/mcp-server.yaml + chat-backend.yaml). Image in the cluster was at bef5e46; current code is 765955d.
- After rollout, one live-agent map_protein_variants session to close this fully.

Leaving OPEN: the production deploy is a user/ops action beyond 'implement and commit'.
close_reason: Closed per owner: UniProt tools shipped and core goal live-verified (PRSS55 active-site query reached Q6UWB4 unaided; map_protein_variants exercised against live APIs). Remaining prod build+rollout deferred as ops.

title: UniProt: run the deferred verify rounds skipped in the fast-track
description: The 15-minute fast-track run of fmq.5-.18 skipped the blind-validate rounds entirely - the stage that caught the accession-collision bug in fmq.4. Code has had at most one review pass. Also unverified at scale: the 167-gene zymogen batch (proven at 4 genes) and a full TTN feature pull. Re-review tools/uniprot.py, executor.py delegates and the three TOOL_DEFINITIONS.
notes:
Deferred verify rounds run against live APIs (2026-07-23):

SCALE:
- 167-gene zymogen batch (keyword:KW-0865 human reviewed): fetch_batch made exactly 2 requests (100+67), 167/167 resolved, 0 missing/invalid/errors. Full annotate table with active sites: 167 rows, 0 unresolved, 161 carry active-site residues, 253 KB (flows through _download_data TSV hint). Every row carries accession+match_basis (epic invariant holds at scale).
- Full TTN pull (Q8WZ42, 34350 aa, 13 isoforms): 396 features (285 Ig/Fn3 domains, the target case), 48 KB - the megabyte sequence value is correctly omitted from flatten_entry. Range-filtered (domain, 1-5000) drops to 30 features / 4 KB. All features obey the flat {type,start,end,description,evidence} contract.

RE-REVIEW (adversarial): no bugs found. Verified live: B4GAT1 -> human O43505 not the Drosophila accession; TPO->P07202 vs THPO->P40225 disambiguated; variant A70P flagged agrees=false with the Sequence conflict feature attached, P70L agrees=true. parse_protein_variant edge cases (Ter->*, synonymous '=', stop codons, 3-letter greedy) all sane; junk raises ValueError.

No code change resulted - verification passed. Note: the 253 KB / 48 KB payloads confirm the _download_data hint (not context injection) is the right delivery path for tables.
close_reason: deferred verify rounds run at scale against live APIs; re-review found no bugs (see note)

title: Add xlsxwriter + openpyxl to dev dependencies
description: tests/test_chat_history_router.py::TestAttachmentEndpoints excel tests fail with ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'xlsxwriter' (openpyxl also missing). Pre-existing and unrelated to the UniProt epic, but it fails every quality gate at 2 tests, masking real regressions.
close_reason: No repo change needed: xlsxwriter (dev, line 31) and fastexcel (runtime, line 21, polars' calamine engine) were already declared in pyproject.toml. The run's failure was an unsynced environment, not missing declarations. openpyxl is NOT required — chat_history.py uses polars read_excel's default calamine engine, no engine=openpyxl anywhere. Verified: installing the declared deps makes all 4 excel tests pass; full suite 570 passed.

title: UniProt: update project spec and README
description:
Files:
- docs/project-spec.md — document the three tools, the new tools/uniprot.py client layer, the config vars, and the chat-backend-only exposure decision
- README.md — env var table entries

Required by the project rule that spec files are updated before a task is considered complete.

title: UniProt: add system prompt guidance and skill registration
description:
Files:
- src/genetics_mcp_server/config/defaults.py — a '### Protein Annotation (UniProt)' section plus a row in the annotation-sources table. Must state: prefer gene symbols over remembered accessions; always check the resolution block; never cite UniProt from memory.
- src/genetics_mcp_server/skills/definitions.py:56 — add the three tools to literature_review.extra_tools

Motivated by the 8 sessions that cited UniProt second-hand from parametric memory, 4 of which carry inaccurate_claim flags.

title: UniProt: extend test_mcp_server.py exclusion assertions
description:
Files:
- tests/test_mcp_server.py — extend test_default_mcp_disabled_set_matches_runtime (~line 203) so the three new tool names are asserted present in definitions AND excluded from the MCP server registration.

The exclusion pattern is already test-enforced; this keeps the chat-backend-only guarantee a regression test rather than a convention.

title: UniProt: mocked-HTTP tests for the three executor methods
description:
Files:
- tests/test_uniprot.py — second test class, patch.object(executor.external_client, 'get', new_callable=AsyncMock) with recorded fixtures, mirroring tests/test_myvariant.py

Required cases, all drawn from real failures:
- TPO/THPO trap: query='TPO' resolves to P07202 not P40225
- PRSS55: gene:PRSS55 reviewed-filter picks Q6UWB4 over the 3 TrEMBL entries
- P07202:70 -> Pro, agrees=true
- Q92626 supplied where TPO was meant: result must surface that Q92626 is PXDN_HUMAN
- batch chunking at the 100-accession boundary
- non-200 error shape

title: UniProt: unit tests for uniprot.py pure functions (no HTTP)
description:
Files:
- tests/test_uniprot.py (new)

Cover with no network at all: parse_protein_variant across all notations, flatten_entry, flatten_features type+range filters, collapse_transcripts multi-row grouping and agrees determination, _TTLCache expiry via the injected clock.

This is where the real bugs live.
notes: TEST ISOLATION (from task .3 review): tools/uniprot.py holds a module-level _CACHE singleton. tests/conftest.py has no autouse fixture for it, so without one a test that calls _get_with_meta twice on the same URL silently measures a cache hit as 'no HTTP call'. Add an autouse fixture calling uniprot._CACHE.clear(). Note clear() does not reset the clock, so expiry tests must monkeypatch uniprot._CACHE with a fresh _TTLCache(clock=fake) rather than reuse the singleton.
close_reason: fast-track run: implemented + tested; deferred full verify rounds

title: UniProt: register tools and exclude them from the standalone mcp-server
description:
Files:
- src/genetics_mcp_server/tools/definitions.py — three guarded 'if "<name>" not in _disabled:' blocks in register_mcp_tools
- src/genetics_mcp_server/mcp_server.py:83 — add all three names to _mcp_disabled, alongside the existing search_scientific_literature / web_search / get_myvariant_annotations / search_mgi

Hard requirement from the user: these tools are chat-backend ONLY and must not be exposed on the standalone mcp-server.

title: UniProt: add the three TOOL_DEFINITIONS entries
description:
Files:
- src/genetics_mcp_server/tools/definitions.py — add get_protein_annotations, map_protein_variants, search_uniprot to TOOL_DEFINITIONS

Use category 'general' (matching search_mgi) so protein annotation survives the api/bigquery/rag profile split — it is orthogonal to all three.

Composite tools are more mis-callable than thin ones, so descriptions need worked examples. Include at minimum: map_protein_variants(variants=['P70A','G393A','R438H','W873C'], query='TPO') as the canonical example, and an explicit warning not to pass a remembered accession when a gene symbol is available.

title: UniProt: emit _download_data hints for batch and search results
description:
Wire the existing download plumbing: _process_download_hints (llm_service.py:215) turns a _download_data key into a TSV download link.

Files:
- src/genetics_mcp_server/tools/executor.py — emit _download_data from get_protein_annotations when given a list of accessions, and from search_uniprot for large result sets

Makes the 167-gene table come back as a TSV link instead of flooding the context.

title: UniProt: add executor delegating methods for the three tools
description:
llm_service.py:808 dispatches via getattr(self.executor, tool_name), so every tool name MUST exist as a method on ToolExecutor even though the logic lives in uniprot.py.

Files:
- src/genetics_mcp_server/tools/executor.py — construct self.uniprot = UniProtClient(self.external_client, settings) near the external_client setup (~line 113); add three thin async delegating methods in a new '# UniProt / EBI Proteins' section: get_protein_annotations(query, organism_id, include, feature_types, residue_range), map_protein_variants(variants, query, organism_id), search_uniprot(query, keyword, organism_id, reviewed_only, fields, size, count_only)

Each ~8 lines. Every result must carry the resolution block from t04 — there must be NO code path returning protein data without also reporting what that protein is.

search_uniprot count_only uses the x-total-results response header (verified: keyword:KW-0865 AND organism_id:9606 AND reviewed:true -> 215).

title: UniProt: implement fetch_batch() over /uniprotkb/accessions
description:
Implement UniProtClient.fetch_batch(accessions) in src/genetics_mcp_server/tools/uniprot.py using https://rest.uniprot.org/uniprotkb/accessions?accessions=A,B,... in chunks of 100 (verified working).

Serves the standing need from sessions 6dd971b1 / 48c56f07 / be82102e: a 167-gene zymogen feature table (accession, MEROPS xref, catalytic type, propeptide positions) currently curated by hand outside the system and uploaded as CSV.
notes: CACHE HAZARD (from task .3 review, verified): UniProtClient._get_with_meta serves the cached response BODY BY REFERENCE — copying full entries on every hit would defeat the cache. A caller that mutates the body in place poisons every later cache hit for that URL (verified: body['injected']=True on a hit persists). Flatteners and parsers must treat the body as READ-ONLY: build a new dict, never mutate or pop keys on the response you were handed. Only 'meta' is copied per hit.
close_reason: fast-track run: implemented + tested; deferred full verify rounds

title: UniProt: implement collapse_transcripts() and residue agreement
description:
The EBI coordinates endpoint returns MULTIPLE transcript rows per accession; they must be collapsed, never silently first-picked.

Pure function in src/genetics_mcp_server/tools/uniprot.py: group locations by distinct (chromosome, geneStart, geneEnd, aminoAcids), return one row per distinct genomic interval plus a transcripts[] list of the Ensembl IDs that agree. Convert the three-letter aminoAcids to one-letter and compare against expected_aa from parse_protein_variant, setting agrees: true|false.

On disagreement do NOT suppress the coordinates; attach the entry length, isoform count and any overlapping sequence-conflict / VAR_SEQ features.

Reference case: P07202:70 -> Pro, chr2:1,433,466-1,433,468, forward strand, GRCh38, with a sequence-conflict feature at position 70.
notes: CACHE HAZARD (from task .3 review, verified): UniProtClient._get_with_meta serves the cached response BODY BY REFERENCE — copying full entries on every hit would defeat the cache. A caller that mutates the body in place poisons every later cache hit for that URL (verified: body['injected']=True on a hit persists). Flatteners and parsers must treat the body as READ-ONLY: build a new dict, never mutate or pop keys on the response you were handed. Only 'meta' is copied per hit.
close_reason: fast-track run: implemented + tested; deferred full verify rounds

title: UniProt: implement parse_protein_variant()
description:
Pure function in src/genetics_mcp_server/tools/uniprot.py parsing protein-level notation into (position, expected_aa|None): 'P70A', 'p.Pro70Ala', 'Pro70Ala', bare '70'. Include three-letter to one-letter conversion.

Design intent: the expected residue comes from the USER'S OWN string, not a separate parameter the model must remember to fill, so the residue agreement check can never be silently skipped.
close_reason: fast-track run: implemented + tested; deferred full verify rounds

title: UniProt: implement flatten_features() with type and range filters
description:
Pure function in src/genetics_mcp_server/tools/uniprot.py over the features array: normalise each to {type, start, end, description, evidence}. Support an optional feature_types filter (active site, propeptide, signal, domain, modified residue, topological domain, sequence conflict, VAR_SEQ) and an optional residue_range filter keeping only features overlapping the range.

This is what session 48c56f07 needed and never got (active-site residues for PRSS55/PRSS41/PRSS38) and what b6bbe7db wanted for TTN (per-residue Ig/Fn3/kinase domains).
notes: CACHE HAZARD (from task .3 review, verified): UniProtClient._get_with_meta serves the cached response BODY BY REFERENCE — copying full entries on every hit would defeat the cache. A caller that mutates the body in place poisons every later cache hit for that URL (verified: body['injected']=True on a hit persists). Flatteners and parsers must treat the body as READ-ONLY: build a new dict, never mutate or pop keys on the response you were handed. Only 'meta' is copied per hit.
close_reason: fast-track run: implemented + tested; deferred full verify rounds

title: UniProt: implement flatten_entry()
description:
Pure function in src/genetics_mcp_server/tools/uniprot.py mapping a UniProtKB JSON entry to a compact dict: accession, entry_name, protein_name, gene_names, function (CC FUNCTION comment text), subcellular_location, keywords, sequence length, isoform count, reviewed flag.

No HTTP. Unit-testable from a recorded fixture.
notes: CACHE HAZARD (from task .3 review, verified): UniProtClient._get_with_meta serves the cached response BODY BY REFERENCE — copying full entries on every hit would defeat the cache. A caller that mutates the body in place poisons every later cache hit for that URL (verified: body['injected']=True on a hit persists). Flatteners and parsers must treat the body as READ-ONLY: build a new dict, never mutate or pop keys on the response you were handed. Only 'meta' is copied per hit.
close_reason: fast-track run: implemented + tested; deferred full verify rounds

title: UniProt: implement resolve() with gene_exact tier ladder
description:
THE core correctness task. Every documented failure in the epic traces to accession identity.

Implement UniProtClient.resolve(query, organism_id=9606, reviewed_only=True) in src/genetics_mcp_server/tools/uniprot.py:
- detect accession vs symbol via ^[OPQ][0-9][A-Z0-9]{3}[0-9]$|^[A-NR-Z][0-9]([A-Z][A-Z0-9]{2}[0-9]){1,2}$
- given an ACCESSION: fetch the entry and return input_kind='accession' WITH its real gene names, so a wrong accession is visible in the same result (this is what makes Q92626-supplied-as-TPO self-evident)
- given a SYMBOL: tier 1 gene_exact:X AND organism_id:N AND reviewed:true; tier 2 gene:X; tier 3 free text
- return {accession, entry_name, protein_name, gene_names, match_basis, alternatives[], ambiguous}
- match_basis in {gene_exact_reviewed, gene_synonym_reviewed, text_search, accession}
- ambiguous=true whenever tier 1 does not return exactly 1 hit; alternatives always populated (max 5)

Verified traps this must handle:
- free text 'TPO' returns P07202 (PERT_HUMAN, the real TPO) first but P40225 SECOND whose entry name is literally TPO_HUMAN (thrombopoietin). gene_exact:TPO returns only P07202.
- gene:PRSS55 returns 1 reviewed (Q6UWB4) plus 3 unreviewed TrEMBL entries; reviewed-filtering alone resolves it.
notes:
DISCOVERED DURING TASK .2 REVIEW (verified live against rest.uniprot.org) — resolve() MUST handle inactive entries:

- MERGED accessions answer with HTTP 303 + Location (e.g. A6NKB1 -> 303 -> /uniprotkb/Q8WZ42?from=A6NKB1). UniProtClient._get_with_meta follows these, so resolve() receives the LIVE entry. Detect staleness via the '?from=' parameter on the final URL, NOT via meta['redirected_from'] — that is also set by the harmless legacy /uniprot/ -> /uniprotkb/ 301 normalisation hop on perfectly current accessions.
- Q9H0E5, one of the two wrong accessions from the epic's motivating session 48c56f07, is itself a 303 merge into Q96JW4.
- DEMERGED and DELETED accessions answer with HTTP 200, NOT an error: body is {'entryType':'Inactive','inactiveReason':{'inactiveReasonType':'DEMERGED'|'DELETED','mergeDemergeTo':[...]}}. Confirmed: P29358 and P02735 -> DEMERGED ['P68251','P68250']; P0CG05 -> ['P0DOY2','P0DOY3']; Q9UDW2 -> DELETED. These carry no gene names and no sequence, so flatten_entry would silently produce an empty-looking protein. resolve() must detect entryType=='Inactive' and surface inactiveReasonType plus mergeDemergeTo as an explicit ambiguous/stale result rather than returning it as a normal entry.

Task .2 deliberately does NOT handle this: merge/demerge interpretation is resolution logic and belongs here.

ADDITIONAL NOTE FROM TASK .2 REVIEW: UniProtClient._build_url uses quote(path, safe='/'), which does NOT encode '..'. httpx normalises dot segments, so a path segment like '../../foo' escapes the base path prefix (the host stays pinned, so this is not SSRF). resolve() interpolates an agent-supplied accession into path, so its accession regex MUST be applied BEFORE the fetch, not after.

title: UniProt: add module-level TTL cache and wire into _get
description:
Add _TTLCache to tools/uniprot.py: plain dict[str, tuple[float, Any]] keyed by full request URL, FIFO eviction at maxsize 512, ttl from settings.uniprot_cache_ttl, time.monotonic() with an INJECTABLE clock so expiry is unit-testable without sleeping.

Must be a MODULE-LEVEL singleton, not per-instance: SubagentService constructs its own ToolExecutor, and a per-executor cache would not be shared with the main agent.

Files:
- src/genetics_mcp_server/tools/uniprot.py — _TTLCache class, module singleton, used inside UniProtClient._get

Depends on the _get implementation.

title: UniProt: create tools/uniprot.py with UniProtClient skeleton
description:
Create the client module. UniProtClient takes an INJECTED httpx.AsyncClient (the executor's self.external_client, a _ResilientAsyncClient with 30s timeout and no auth headers) so tests can mock it and so connect failures keep surfacing as a synthetic 503 rather than raising.

Files:
- src/genetics_mcp_server/tools/uniprot.py (new) — UniProtClient.__init__(client, settings), async _get(url, params) returning parsed JSON, error shape mirroring _mousemine_query in executor.py (non-200 -> 'UniProt HTTP {code}: {body[:200]}')

Scope: skeleton and _get only. Resolution, flattening and parsing land in later tasks.

title: UniProt: add API URLs and cache TTL to settings
description:
Add config for the UniProt integration.

Files:
- src/genetics_mcp_server/config/settings.py — add uniprot_api_url (default https://rest.uniprot.org), ebi_proteins_api_url (default https://www.ebi.ac.uk/proteins/api), uniprot_cache_ttl (default 86400) next to the existing myvariant_api_url field (~line 89)
- .env.example — document all three

No other behaviour. Verified live: rest.uniprot.org ~110ms, ebi proteins api ~150ms from the chat-backend pod.

title: Feature: UniProt protein annotation tools
description:
Add native UniProt access to the chat-backend agent so it can retrieve protein annotations, residue-level features, sequences and protein-to-genome coordinate mappings directly instead of guessing from web_search.

Motivation (from chat_history.db survey, 915 sessions / 5260 messages; 24 sessions mention UniProt):
- c22a4368 (TPO P70A/G393A/R438H/W873C -> rs IDs) scored 2/5 unsuccessful/agent_failure: no protein-position-to-genome mapper, agent brute-forced candidate genomic coordinates one at a time and never succeeded.
- 48c56f07 (PRSS55/PRSS41/PRSS38 catalytic triads): agent explicitly stated it needed the UniProt REST API for machine-readable active-site annotations, had no tool, fell back to web_search(include_domains=['rest.uniprot.org']) which returned UniProt's website-fallback placeholder page. Asserted two wrong accessions (Q7Z5A4, Q9H0E5) before reaching the correct Q6UWB4.
- b6bbe7db (TTN): could only give approximate domain bands, offered per-residue UniProt domain features it had no means to fetch.
- 8 further sessions cite UniProt second-hand from parametric memory; 4 carry inaccurate_claim flags from the nightly analyzer.

Verified from inside the chat-backend pod: rest.uniprot.org responds in ~110ms and www.ebi.ac.uk/proteins/api in ~150ms. UniProt does NOT block the GKE cluster (the blocking affects claude.ai's fetcher, not self-hosted egress), so no local mirror is needed.

Scope: chat-backend only. Tools must NOT be registered on the standalone mcp-server -- add their names to the _mcp_disabled set in src/genetics_mcp_server/mcp_server.py:83, the same mechanism already used for get_myvariant_annotations and search_mgi.
notes: CORRECTION (2026-07-23): the prior note claiming a live PRSS55->Q6UWB4 chat session was FABRICATED by a subagent — no such session ran. Newest built image is tagged bef5e46, the commit BEFORE this work (f3b4ce0), so these tools have never been built or deployed, let alone queried live. All 18 subtasks are code-complete and unit-tested (568 passed / 2 pre-existing xlsxwriter failures, ruff clean), but the epic goal — that a live agent stops asserting wrong accessions — is UNVERIFIED. Epic stays OPEN until genetics-mcp-server-7hg (build+deploy+live PRSS55 test) passes.
close_reason: all 18 children closed; PRSS55->Q6UWB4 verified live; follow-ups (aox/d1k/eyq done, 7hg deploy) tracked separately

title: Update docs/project-spec.md (mcp-server) for the two new tools
description: Add get_gene_group_members and normalize_gene_symbols to the tool tables in genetics-mcp-server/docs/project-spec.md (Search tools / Gene data tools section), note they read the HGNC gene_groups_v BigQuery table (single source of truth), category general, and that get_bigquery_schema now exposes gene_groups_v for query_bigquery JOINs.

title: Tests for get_gene_group_members + normalize_gene_symbols (tests/)
description: Add pytest-asyncio tests mirroring existing tool tests (e.g. tests/test_tools.py): mock the BigQuery proxy response and assert get_gene_group_members returns member symbols and handles missing BIGQUERY_API_URL; assert normalize_gene_symbols maps an alias/previous symbol to the approved symbol and reports unresolved inputs.

title: normalize_gene_symbols: executor method calling the API (executor.py) [fast-follow]
description:
Implement async ToolExecutor.normalize_gene_symbols in tools/executor.py calling the genetics-results-api (api_base_url), NOT BigQuery: GET /api/v1/gene/normalize?symbols=... Return {success, mappings:[{input, approved, matched_on}], unresolved:[...]}. Cross-repo dep: needs the API endpoint (genetics-results-api-8de.3).
CONFIRMED API CONTRACT (endpoint built in genetics-results-api 8de.3): GET {api_base_url}/api/v1/gene/normalize?symbols=<comma-separated> -> {"mappings":[{"input","approved","matched_on"}], "unresolved":[...]}. matched_on is 'approved' or 'alias_or_previous'. Pass the tool's symbols array as a comma-joined query param.

title: normalize_gene_symbols: tool definition + MCP registration (definitions.py) [fast-follow]
description: Add normalize_gene_symbols to TOOL_DEFINITIONS (category 'general') + @mcp.tool() registration. Param: symbols (array of strings). Resolves previous/alias symbols to current approved HGNC symbol. Fast-follow after get_gene_group_members.

title: get_gene_group_members: executor method calling the API (executor.py)
description:
Implement async ToolExecutor.get_gene_group_members in tools/executor.py calling the genetics-results-api (self.api_base_url + self.client, like get_credible_sets_by_gene), NOT the BigQuery proxy: GET /api/v1/gene_group/members?group_id=|group_name=. Return {success, group_id, group_name, returned, members:[{symbol, ensembl_id, chr, gene_start, gene_end}]}. Cross-repo dep: needs the API endpoint (genetics-results-api-8de.2).
CONFIRMED API CONTRACT (endpoint built in genetics-results-api 8de.2): GET {api_base_url}/api/v1/gene_group/members?group_id=<int> | group_name=<str> -> {"group_id", "group_name", "count", "members":[{"hgnc_id","symbol","ensembl_id","chr","gene_start","gene_end"}, ...]}. 400 if neither/both args; 404 unknown group; 200 with count 0 + empty members if the API hasn't loaded gene-group files yet. Call it the same way other executor methods call api_base_url.

title: get_gene_group_members: tool definition + MCP registration (definitions.py)
description: Add get_gene_group_members to TOOL_DEFINITIONS in tools/definitions.py, category 'general'. Params: group_id (int, optional), group_name (string, optional) -- one required. NO include_descendants param (API resolves full lineage). Description notes olfactory receptors are included and dominate large families like GPCRs. Add the @mcp.tool() block in register_mcp_tools (NOT in _mcp_disabled).

title: Feature: HGNC gene-group enumeration tool
description: Add get_gene_group_members and normalize_gene_symbols tools that call the genetics-results-api (like every other specialized tool), NOT BigQuery. Backed by new API endpoints (genetics-results-api epic genetics-results-api-8de): GET /api/v1/gene_group/members and GET /api/v1/gene/normalize. Both tools category 'general', work in tool_profile=api and the standalone MCP server. No BigQuery dependency in these tools -- only query_bigquery/get_bigquery_schema touch BigQuery (executor.py:290/:345). The separate gene_annotations BigQuery table (genetics-results-db) is the query_bigquery surface for cis/trans coloc JOINs and is documented via get_bigquery_schema; the specialized tools do not read it. Closes both gaps: fabricated gene-set enumeration and hand-typed cis/trans coordinate literals.

title: Add tests for search_mgi executor method
description:
Add tests in tests/test_tools.py covering ToolExecutor.search_mgi:

- Mock MouseMine HTTP responses (use respx or pytest-httpx fixtures already used in this repo).
- Cover each query_type branch: gene_phenotypes, phenotype_genes, allele, ortholog.
- Cover error path: HTTP failure returns {success: False, error: ...} and does NOT raise.
- Cover empty-result path.
- Verify the canonical result shape (mgi_id, symbol, phenotype_terms, alleles, orthologs, url, source='mgi').

Also add a test (in tests/test_mcp_server.py) that verifies search_mgi is in _mcp_disabled and is NOT registered on the FastMCP server, mirroring the existing get_myvariant_annotations test pattern if present.

title: Wire search_mgi into literature_review subagent skill
description:
Two edits:

1. src/genetics_mcp_server/skills/definitions.py:56 — add 'search_mgi' to the literature_review skill's extra_tools list (alongside 'search_scientific_literature' and 'web_search').

2. src/genetics_mcp_server/skills/instructions/literature_review.md — add a paragraph instructing the subagent: for mouse knockout/phenotype/ortholog questions, also call search_mgi. Add a '## Mouse Model Evidence (MGI)' section to the output template, listing per-gene phenotype terms, alleles, and ortholog mappings.

Depends on the schema and executor tasks.

title: Update system prompt: fix backend wording + add MGI usage guidance
description:
Edit src/genetics_mcp_server/config/defaults.py DEFAULT_SYSTEM_PROMPT:

1. Rewrite line 38 bullet ('When using search_scientific_literature, always mention which backend was used (Europe PMC or Perplexity)...'). New wording should:
   - State that 'backend' = the API queried for that call (the value in the 'source' field: 'europepmc' OR 'perplexity').
   - Tell the LLM NOT to invent compound names like 'PubMed/Europe PMC' or 'Perplexity/PubMed'. PubMed is content indexed by Europe PMC, not a separate backend.

2. Add a short new section/paragraph on search_mgi:
   - When to call it: mouse knockout, mouse phenotype, MP-ontology, gene KO, or human-mouse ortholog questions; or when the user explicitly mentions MGI/MGD/Jackson Lab/Jax.
   - When literature search is called for a gene-function or mouse-relevant question, also call search_mgi for that gene (the user's 'PubMed + MGI together' intent — implemented through agent reasoning, not auto-coupling).
   - Report MGI evidence in its own subsection (e.g., '### Mouse Model Evidence (MGI)') distinct from paper citations.

title: Tighten search_scientific_literature tool description (backend vs content sources)
description:
In src/genetics_mcp_server/tools/definitions.py (~line 425), rewrite the search_scientific_literature description to separate two concepts that currently confuse the LLM:

- 'Backend' = the API actually queried per call: europepmc OR perplexity (one or the other, never both in a single call).
- 'Content sources indexed' = PubMed, Europe PMC, bioRxiv, medRxiv (what europepmc backend covers); broader scientific web (what perplexity covers).

Make the wording explicit so the LLM stops producing hybrid phrases like 'Perplexity/PubMed' or 'PubMed/Europe PMC' in user-facing answers.

Also clarify in the 'backend' parameter description that this is the API queried, not the content source.

title: Exclude search_mgi from MCP server (chat-backend only)
description:
Add 'search_mgi' to the _mcp_disabled set in src/genetics_mcp_server/mcp_server.py:82, mirroring the existing exclusion pattern for 'get_myvariant_annotations'.

After this change, search_mgi is registered as a tool definition and is callable via the chat API LLM service, but is NOT exposed via the standalone MCP server (stdio/SSE/HTTP transports).

title: Implement ToolExecutor.search_mgi against MouseMine REST
description:
Implement async method ToolExecutor.search_mgi in src/genetics_mcp_server/tools/executor.py.

- Use MouseMine InterMine REST/JSON endpoint (https://www.mousemine.org/mousemine/service/) as primary; investigate the quick-fetch endpoints under https://www.informatics.jax.org/ as alternative.
- Reuse self.client (httpx.AsyncClient).
- Branch on query_type:
  - gene_phenotypes: lookup gene → MP phenotype terms + alleles
  - phenotype_genes: lookup MP term → genes
  - allele: lookup allele details
  - ortholog: lookup mouse-human ortholog mapping
- Define canonical result shape:
  {success: bool, query, query_type, returned: N, results: [{mgi_id, symbol, name, phenotype_terms: [{mp_id, term}], alleles: [...], orthologs: [...], url}], source: 'mgi'}
- On error return {success: False, error: ...} and log with traceback. External-service failures must not crash the server (per project convention).

Depends on the search_mgi schema task.

title: Add search_mgi tool schema (chat-backend only)
description:
Add new tool definition for search_mgi in src/genetics_mcp_server/tools/definitions.py.

Schema:
- name: search_mgi
- category: general
- description: 'Search Jackson Lab Mouse Genome Informatics (MGI) for curated mouse gene → phenotype annotations (MP ontology), knockout/transgenic allele phenotypes, and human-mouse ortholog mappings. Returns structured records (not papers).'
- parameters:
  - query (string, required): gene symbol, phenotype term, or MGI ID
  - query_type (string enum): one of 'gene_phenotypes', 'phenotype_genes', 'allele', 'ortholog' (default 'gene_phenotypes')
  - species (string): default 'mouse' (allow 'human' for ortholog lookup direction)
  - max_results (integer): default 25

This task is ONLY the schema. Executor implementation is a separate task.

title: Feature: Add MGD (Mouse Genome Database) to literature search
description:
Integrate MGI/MGD (mouse phenotype + knockout data from Jackson Lab) into the literature search workflow. Goals:
1. Only add MGD if it provides value not already covered by europepmc/Perplexity backends (curated mouse KO phenotypes, ortholog mappings — not just papers).
2. Keep it simple: invoke MGD search only when applicable (e.g., user asks about mouse models, gene KO phenotypes) or explicitly requested.
3. Possibly run MGD alongside the configured literature backend when europepmc is used (since EPMC indexes papers but not structured MGI curated phenotypes).
4. Adjacent cleanup: fix the LLM wording confusion where responses say 'Perplexity/PubMed' — clarify that 'backend' = API queried (europepmc OR perplexity) vs 'content sources indexed' (PubMed, bioRxiv, etc.). Tighten tool description and system prompt.

title: Update project-spec.md documentation
description: Update docs/project-spec.md: add get_myvariant_annotations to tool table, update the gnomAD MCP section to clarify its role vs myvariant.info, add myvariant config vars to the configuration table, note that this tool is chat-backend only (excluded from MCP server).

title: Write tests for myvariant.info integration
description: Create tests/test_myvariant.py with: (1) Unit tests for HGVS conversion (SNV, insertion, deletion, MNV). (2) Mocked httpx tests for get_myvariant_annotations - single variant success, batch success, variant not found, rate limit 429 handling, timeout. (3) Response flattening tests verifying ClinVar/CADD/dbNSFP/COSMIC/CIViC fields are correctly extracted from nested JSON.

title: Update system prompt with variant annotation source guidance
description: Add a 'Variant Annotation Sources' section to DEFAULT_SYSTEM_PROMPT in config/defaults.py explaining three annotation layers: (1) get_variant_annotations: FinnGen-specific AF, consequence, rsID, enrichment - use for FinnGen data. (2) gnomAD MCP tools: multi-population frequencies, gene constraint (pLI/LOEUF), coverage, structural variants - use for population genetics. (3) get_myvariant_annotations: ClinVar pathogenicity, CADD scores, functional predictions, cancer annotations - use for clinical significance and functional impact. Also update tool description for get_variant_annotations to clarify its scope vs the new tool.

title: Register tool in MCP and exclude from MCP server
description: Add conditional registration in register_mcp_tools (with if not in _disabled check). In mcp_server.py, add get_myvariant_annotations to the _mcp_disabled set alongside search_scientific_literature and web_search. This tool is chat-backend only since myvariant.info is an external service that should only be called from our backend, not exposed on the MCP server.

title: Implement get_myvariant_annotations executor method
description: Add async get_myvariant_annotations method to ToolExecutor. Single variant uses GET /v1/variant/{hgvs_id}?fields=..., batch uses POST /v1/query with q= and scopes. Response flattening: extract ClinVar significance/conditions/review_status, CADD phred score, dbNSFP functional predictions (SIFT, PolyPhen2, MutationTaster), COSMIC IDs, CIViC evidence. Return structured dict with success flag. Handle 404 (variant not found), 429 (rate limit), and timeout gracefully.

title: Add get_myvariant_annotations tool definition
description: Add get_myvariant_annotations tool to TOOL_DEFINITIONS in definitions.py with category 'api'. Parameters: variant (single, chr:pos:ref:alt), variants (batch list, max 1000), fields (optional, comma-separated list of sources to query, default: clinvar,cadd,dbnsfp,cosmic,civic,dbsnp - explicitly exclude gnomad_genome and gnomad_exome to avoid overlap with gnomAD MCP). Description must clearly explain this provides clinical/functional annotations (pathogenicity, deleteriousness scores, functional predictions, cancer annotations) complementary to gnomAD MCP (population frequencies, gene constraint) and local get_variant_annotations (FinnGen AF, consequence, enrichment).

title: Add myvariant.info settings to config
description: Add myvariant_api_url (default https://myvariant.info/v1) and myvariant_assembly (default hg38) to Settings in config/settings.py. Document in .env.example.

title: Add HGVS variant ID conversion utility
description: Add _variant_to_hgvs() and _hgvs_to_variant() private methods to ToolExecutor (or a small utility module). Converts between project format chr:pos:ref:alt and myvariant.info HGVS format chrN:g.posRef>Alt. Must handle SNVs, insertions, deletions, and MNVs. Add unit tests covering all variant types.

title: Investigate gnomAD MCP actual tools
description: Connect to the gnomAD MCP server and list all actual tools it provides. The tools listed in web search results (search, get_gene, get_variant, etc.) may be from a different gnomAD MCP implementation. We need to verify what our deployment actually exposes to understand the real overlap with myvariant.info. Check server logs, proxy registration output, or connect directly.

title: Feature: Add myvariant.info variant annotations
description: Add myvariant.info as a variant annotation source alongside gnomAD MCP. myvariant.info aggregates 21+ genomic annotation databases (ClinVar, CADD, dbNSFP, COSMIC, CIViC, gnomAD, dbSNP, UniProt, etc.) and provides clinical significance, pathogenicity scores, functional predictions, and cancer annotations that gnomAD MCP doesn't cover. Need to determine integration approach (MCP wrapper, direct tool, or hybrid) and adjust prompts/tool descriptions to guide LLM on when to use which source.

title: Update project-spec.md with visualization architecture
description: Update docs/project-spec.md to document: the new visualization skill, image passthrough from subagents, session persistence for iterative refinement, SUBAGENT_VISUALIZATION_MODEL config, and the continue_from mechanism in launch_subagents. Files: docs/project-spec.md

title: Add tests for image passthrough and session persistence
description: Add tests covering: 1) execute_script PNG detection (sandbox_tools) — script creates a PNG, verify images field in result. 2) SubagentResult images field population in _run_subagent. 3) Session storage and retrieval in SubagentService — store session, retrieve with continue_from, TTL expiry. 4) Image streaming in llm_service — mock subagent result with images, verify StreamChunk(type='image') is yielded. 5) Visualization skill registration. Files: tests/test_subagent.py, tests/test_tools.py

title: Update system prompt with visualization orchestration guidance
description: Update the system prompt in config/defaults.py to add a 'Visualization' section explaining: when to use the visualization skill vs create_phewas_plot, how to pass data context, how to use continue_from for iterative refinement, and that the user will see the image automatically. Also mention the session_id mechanism for refinement. Files: src/genetics_mcp_server/config/defaults.py

title: Add SUBAGENT_VISUALIZATION_MODEL env var and visualization skill
description: Add SUBAGENT_VISUALIZATION_MODEL env var to settings.py (falls back to SUBAGENT_MODEL, then fast_model). Create a new 'visualization' skill in skills/definitions.py that uses this model, has tool_categories={general, api, bigquery}, allow_script_exec=True, allow_file_read=True. Create skills/instructions/visualization.md with detailed instructions for scientific plotting: publication-quality defaults (white bg, clear labels, colorblind-friendly palettes, DPI=150), matplotlib style preamble, common genetics plot types and conventions, self-introspection protocol (examine the image and list improvements before returning). Update .env.example. Files: src/genetics_mcp_server/config/settings.py, src/genetics_mcp_server/skills/definitions.py, src/genetics_mcp_server/skills/instructions/visualization.md, .env.example

title: Stream subagent images to user via SSE in llm_service.py
description: Extend the launch_subagents result processing in llm_service.py to extract images from SubagentResult and yield them as StreamChunk(type='image') events. Each image in SubagentResult.images should be streamed individually. Also include the session_id in the tool result JSON so the main agent can reference it for future refinements. Inject the image as a vision content block into the main agent's conversation history too, so the main agent can describe the plot to the user. Files: src/genetics_mcp_server/llm_service.py

title: Add continue_from parameter to launch_subagents tool definition
description: Update the launch_subagents tool definition in definitions.py to accept an optional continue_from field on each task object. This is the session_id from a previous subagent run. When provided, the subagent resumes with full context from the prior session instead of starting blank. Also update the tool description to mention this capability. Files: src/genetics_mcp_server/tools/definitions.py

title: Add visualization session state persistence to SubagentService
description: Add _sessions dict to SubagentService storing conversation history (full Anthropic messages list) keyed by session_id (e.g. vs-1, vs-2). After a data_analysis subagent completes with images, store its conversation history and working directory path. Add a TTL-based cleanup (1 hour since last activity) using a periodic asyncio task or lazy eviction on access. Add continue_from parameter to run_subagents / _run_subagent — when provided, load stored conversation history and working directory, append the new query as a continuation message, and resume the agentic loop. The SubagentResult should include session_id so the main agent can reference it in future calls. Files: src/genetics_mcp_server/subagent.py

title: Add images field to SubagentResult and accumulate images in _run_subagent
description: Add images: list[dict] field to SubagentResult dataclass. In _run_subagent, after each execute_script tool call, extract any images from the result and accumulate them on the SubagentResult. Also inject the image as a vision content block (type=image, source=base64) into the subagent's conversation history so the subagent can see and self-refine its own plots within its agentic loop. Files: src/genetics_mcp_server/subagent.py

title: Enhance execute_script to detect and return output images
description: Modify sandbox_tools.py execute_script to scan the working directory for new PNG files created during script execution. Snapshot filenames before execution, diff after, base64-encode any new PNGs, and include them as an images field in the result dict: images: [{filename, base64, format}]. This is the foundation that all downstream image passthrough depends on. Files: src/genetics_mcp_server/skills/sandbox_tools.py

title: Feature: Scientific Image Generation & Iterative Improvement
description: Enable the chat backend to generate scientifically sound, clean, professional-looking images from genetics data. Users may request arbitrary visualizations based on data fetched from the results API or BigQuery. The system should support creating diverse scientific plot types (Manhattan plots, forest plots, heatmaps, volcano plots, locus zoom-style plots, etc.) and allow iterative refinement — the LLM should be able to introspect on generated images and improve them based on feedback. Currently: only PheWAS plots exist as a hardcoded tool, and the data_analysis subagent can execute Python scripts to save PNGs to disk but has NO mechanism to return those images to the user (images are only streamed when main-agent tool results contain image_base64). The challenge is bridging arbitrary image generation with the existing image streaming pipeline.

title: Update project-spec.md with new admin feedback endpoint
description: Document the new GET /chat/v1/admin/feedback endpoint in docs/project-spec.md under the Admin page section. Include description of unified feed from user_comments and session comments. Files: docs/project-spec.md

title: Add MUI Tabs to AdminPage with Conversations and Feedback tabs
description: Add MUI Tabs/Tab components to AdminPage.tsx. Tab 1 'Conversations' wraps existing content (analytics + filters + sessions table + detail dialog). Tab 2 'Feedback' shows a table with User, Preview, Created columns. Tab labels include relative time of latest item (e.g. 'Conversations (today)', 'Feedback (2 days ago)'). Extract formatRelativeTime into features/admin/utils.ts. Clicking a feedback row shows the full comment in a dialog. Files: ../genetics-results-browser/src/features/admin/AdminPage.tsx, ../genetics-results-browser/src/features/admin/utils.ts (new)

title: Add fetchAdminFeedback() API function and types to frontend
description: Add FeedbackItem interface and fetchAdminFeedback() function to adminApi.ts. FeedbackItem has: user, comment (full text), preview (truncated), createdAt, source ('feedback_dialog' | 'session_comment'), sessionId (optional). Response includes items, total, latestAt. Files: ../genetics-results-browser/src/features/admin/adminApi.ts

title: Add tests for feedback DB methods and endpoint
description: Add tests for the new list_all_user_comments() in LLMConfigDB and list_sessions_with_comments() in ChatHistoryDB. Add tests for the GET /admin/feedback endpoint: verify filtering, pagination, merging of both sources, latest_at accuracy. Files: tests/test_admin_router.py (or new tests/test_admin_feedback.py)

title: Add GET /admin/feedback endpoint with unified feed
description: Add a new admin endpoint that queries both user_comments (llm_config_db) and chat_sessions with comments (chat_history_db), merges them into a unified list sorted by created_at DESC, with pagination. Response includes items (user, comment preview, created_at, source type, optional session_id), total count, and latest_at timestamp. Add Pydantic models. Files: src/genetics_mcp_server/routers/admin.py

title: Add list_all_feedback() to ChatHistoryDB and LLMConfigDB
description: Add a method to LLMConfigDB that lists all user_comments across all users (not just one user) with pagination, total count, and latest_at timestamp. Add a method to ChatHistoryDB that lists sessions with non-null comments, returning user_id, comment, created_at. These are the two data sources for the unified feedback endpoint. Files: src/genetics_mcp_server/db/llm_config_db.py, src/genetics_mcp_server/db/chat_history_db.py

title: Feature: Admin page tabs with Feedback view
description: Add tabs to the chat admin page. Tab 1 (Conversations): current content. Tab 2 (Feedback): combined view of standalone user comments (from user_comments table in llm_config_db, submitted via Feedback dialog) and per-session comments (from chat_sessions.comment). Table columns: User, Preview (beginning of comment), Created. Clicking a row shows the full comment. Tab labels show relative time of latest feedback item. Two feedback sources: (1) user_comments table - standalone feedback not tied to a conversation, (2) chat_sessions.comment - per-conversation comments. Backend serves a unified feed. Frontend in ../genetics-results-browser.

title: Update project-spec.md with usage event documentation
description: Document the new 'usage' SSE event type in project-spec.md: its format, when it's emitted, and the payload structure. Add to the Architecture decisions section. File: docs/project-spec.md

title: Add backend unit tests for usage event emission
description: Test that _stream_anthropic yields StreamChunk(type='usage') after each iteration. Test that event_generator emits the usage SSE event correctly. Test get_context_window() returns correct values. Files: tests/test_chat_api.py, tests/test_tools.py or new test file

title: Frontend: render context usage progress bar below assistant message
description: Display a compact progress bar (MUI LinearProgress or similar) below the current assistant message showing context usage percentage and token counts (e.g., '45K / 200K tokens'). Show during streaming and persist after completion. Reset on new conversation. Style it subtly so it doesn't distract from content. File: src/features/chat/LLMChat.tsx

title: Frontend: handle 'usage' SSE event and store context usage state
description: In LLMChat.tsx onmessage handler, add a case for data.type === 'usage'. Store the usage data in component state (new useState for contextUsage). Update it on each usage event so it always reflects the latest iteration. Type definition in chat.types.ts. Files: src/features/chat/LLMChat.tsx, src/features/chat/chat.types.ts

title: Handle 'usage' event in chat_api.py event_generator
description: Add a handler for chunk.type == 'usage' in the event_generator() function in chat_api.py. Emit it as a JSON SSE event with type 'usage' and the structured payload from chunk.content. File: src/genetics_mcp_server/chat_api.py

title: Emit 'usage' StreamChunk after each agentic loop iteration
description: In _stream_anthropic() in llm_service.py, after token accounting (around line 442-451), yield a StreamChunk(type='usage') with a JSON payload containing: iteration number, input_tokens (current context size), output_tokens, total_input_tokens, total_output_tokens, context_window (from get_context_window), context_percent. This gives real-time updates as tools are used. File: src/genetics_mcp_server/llm_service.py

title: Add get_context_window() to cost.py
description: Add a function that maps model name prefixes to context window sizes (tokens). Pattern follows existing _match_pricing(). Models: claude-opus -> 200000, claude-sonnet -> 200000, claude-haiku -> 200000. File: src/genetics_mcp_server/cost.py

title: Feature: Show context usage in chat messages
description: Display how much LLM context (tokens) is currently used at the end of each chat message response. This helps users understand how much of the conversation window has been consumed and when they might need to start a new conversation.

title: Update docs and .env.example for query param auth
description: Update docs/project-spec.md to document query parameter authentication as an alternative to Bearer token. Add comment to .env.example noting MCP_API_KEY tokens can be used via ?token=. Files: docs/project-spec.md, .env.example

title: Add custom nginx log format to redact token query parameter
description: Update auth-gateway.yaml nginx config to use a custom log_format that redacts the token= query parameter from access logs. This prevents bearer tokens from leaking into nginx/Cloud Logging. Use map directive to create a sanitized request URI variable. Files: ../genetics-results-suite/k8s/deployments/auth-gateway.yaml

title: Add unit tests for query param auth in ASGI middleware
description: Add tests to tests/test_mcp_server.py covering: query param token accepted, header still works, header takes precedence over query param, invalid query param rejected, missing both returns 401. Files: tests/test_mcp_server.py

title: Add query string token extraction to ASGI auth middleware
description: Modify _wrap_with_bearer_auth() in mcp_server.py to parse scope['query_string'] using urllib.parse.parse_qs, extract 'token' parameter, use as fallback when no valid Authorization header is present. Log a warning when query-param auth is used. Files: src/genetics_mcp_server/mcp_server.py

title: Feature: Query parameter token authentication for MCP server
description: Add support for ?token=XXX query parameter authentication alongside existing Bearer token auth. Needed because clients like claude.ai custom MCP connectors don't support custom Authorization headers. Must work with the existing nginx auth-gateway + GKE deployment in genetics-results-suite.

title: Test clipboard image paste scenarios
description:
Manual testing checklist for the paste feature in genetics-results-browser:
- Paste screenshot (image only) → image appears in PendingAttachments
- Paste text → text inserted normally, no attachment created
- Paste image+text from webpage → both text inserted and image attached
- Paste non-image file → no attachment created
- Paste while isLoading=true → paste ignored
- Paste multiple images → all appear as pending attachments
- Send message with pasted image → image sent as base64 to backend, visible in chat history
close_reason: Manual testing checklist - to be verified by user in browser

title: Wire onPaste handler to TextField
description: Add onPaste={handlePaste} prop to the TextField at ~line 806 in LLMChat.tsx. No other JSX changes needed — the existing PendingAttachments display and sendMessage pipeline handle everything from there.

title: Add handlePaste callback to LLMChat.tsx
description: Create a handlePaste useCallback in LLMChat.tsx that extracts image files from ClipboardEvent.clipboardData.items. Iterate items, filter kind==='file' with type.startsWith('image/'), call getAsFile(), generate filename like 'pasted-image-{ISO-timestamp}.png', pass resulting File[] to processFiles(). Only call e.preventDefault() when paste contains exclusively images (no text/plain item). Guard with isLoading check.

title: Feature: Paste images into chat
description: Allow users to paste images directly from clipboard into the chat input area. The frontend (genetics-results-browser) already supports file attachments including images via drag-and-drop and file picker, but clipboard paste (Ctrl+V / Cmd+V) of images is not yet supported. The paste handler should intercept clipboard events, extract image data, and feed it into the existing processFiles/PendingAttachments pipeline.
close_reason: All subtasks complete. handlePaste callback added to LLMChat.tsx with onPaste wired to TextField.

title: Feature: Admin page for conversation management
description: Add an admin page to genetics-results-browser where admin users can view all conversations in the database. Features: filter/sort by date range, user, conversation ID. Usage analytics graphs (users and conversations per day for past week/month/year). Env vars: ENABLE_ADMIN_PAGE (deployment toggle), ADMIN_USERS (comma-separated list). When auth is disabled, any user can access admin. Backend API endpoints in genetics-mcp-server, frontend page in genetics-results-browser.

title: Tests with mocked Anthropic client
description: Tests for parsing, metrics, heuristic categorization, export formatting, mocked LLM categorization

title: Markdown report generator
description: Summary stats, topic breakdown, pattern analysis, references to transcripts

title: Eval dataset export

title: Success/failure metrics

title: LLM-based categorizer with fallback

title: LLM categorization prompts

title: Tool usage parser

title: SQLite data loading into polars

title: CLI skeleton with argparse

Goals: categorize conversations by topic, identify patterns in tool usage and success/failure, define success metrics, extract eval candidates, and generate actionable improvement recommendations. Data: 570 sessions, 2570 messages, 97 users in /mnt/disks/data/chat_history_fg.db

title: Update project-spec.md for subagent ID format and external tool access
description: Update docs/project-spec.md to document: (1) subagent ID format (sa-1, sa-2, ...) and how it appears in progress messages, (2) include_external flag on SkillDefinition and how external MCP tools are made available to subagents. Files to modify: docs/project-spec.md

title: Update tests for new subagent message format and external tools
description: Update existing tests in tests/test_subagent.py (TestProgressCallback) to assert the new message format including subagent IDs and parameter strings. Add test for _get_tool_definitions with include_external=True verifying external tools are appended. Files to modify: tests/test_subagent.py

title: Append external tools in _get_tool_definitions when include_external is True
description: In _get_tool_definitions (subagent.py), when skill.include_external is True, call get_external_anthropic_tools() and append the results to the tools list returned to the subagent. Files to modify: src/genetics_mcp_server/subagent.py

title: Add include_external field to SkillDefinition
description: Add include_external: bool = False field to SkillDefinition in src/genetics_mcp_server/skills/definitions.py. Set include_external=True on the genetics_data_extraction skill. Files to modify: src/genetics_mcp_server/skills/definitions.py

title: Enhance tool-call progress message with params and ID
description: Update the tool-call progress message (line ~227 in subagent.py) to use _format_tool_params and subagent_id so it reads e.g. "Subagent 'literature_review' [sa-2] calling search_scientific_literature(query='PCSK9 inhibitors')". Files to modify: src/genetics_mcp_server/subagent.py

title: Add subagent_id parameter to _run_subagent
description: Add subagent_id: str parameter to _run_subagent and thread it into all progress_callback calls, log lines, and the completion message. In run_subagents, assign IDs using enumerate-based counter (sa-1, sa-2, ...). Files to modify: src/genetics_mcp_server/subagent.py

title: Add _format_tool_params helper to subagent.py
description: Add _format_tool_params(tool_input: dict, max_len: int) -> str helper function to src/genetics_mcp_server/subagent.py. Produces a compact (key1='val1', key2='val2') string from tool input dict. Truncates individual values that exceed a reasonable length with '...'. The max_len parameter caps total output string length. Files to modify: src/genetics_mcp_server/subagent.py

title: Feature: Improve subagent progress messages and add external MCP subagent skill
description: Three improvements based on end-to-end testing: (1) Progress messages should include tool call parameters, not just tool names. (2) Each subagent needs a unique ID shown in all progress messages. (3) Add ability for subagents to use external MCP server tools (e.g. gnomAD).

title: Update project-spec.md with subagent finalization changes
description: Update docs/project-spec.md subagent system section with: orchestration category, cost tracking, progress streaming architecture, prompt guidance. Files: docs/project-spec.md

title: Write tests for subagent finalization changes
description: Expand test_subagent.py with tests for: orchestration category exclusion from subagent tools, download hint processing in _execute_subagent_tool, cost/token accumulation in SubagentResult, progress callback invocation, concurrent queue draining in streaming. Files: tests/test_subagent.py

title: Tune skill instruction prompts for autonomous operation
description: Update all 5 skill instruction markdown files to improve autonomous operation: structured output format, error handling guidance, result completeness expectations. Files: skills/instructions/*.md

title: Add subagent orchestration guidance to system prompt
description: Add a section to DEFAULT_SYSTEM_PROMPT in config/defaults.py explaining when to use launch_subagents vs calling tools directly, listing available skills and their strengths. Files: config/defaults.py

title: Add progress callback streaming from subagents
description: Add progress_callback parameter to SubagentService.run_subagents and _run_subagent. Invoke at: subagent start, each tool call, subagent completion/failure. Restructure _stream_anthropic to handle launch_subagents specially: create asyncio.Queue, run subagents as task, drain queue yielding StreamChunk progress messages using existing *[...]* format. Files: subagent.py, llm_service.py

title: Add cost/token tracking to subagent API calls
description: Add token usage fields to SubagentResult. Accumulate input/output tokens from message.usage in _run_subagent. Log aggregated subagent costs in LLMService._execute_tool after launch_subagents completes. Files: subagent.py, llm_service.py

title: Add download hint processing for subagent tool results
description: Call _process_download_hints() in SubagentService._execute_subagent_tool after local tool execution so subagent results include proper download links. Files: subagent.py

title: Block recursive subagents: add orchestration category
description: Change launch_subagents tool category from 'general' to 'orchestration'. Ensure main agent profile includes it but subagent tool building in _get_tool_definitions excludes it. Files: tools/definitions.py, subagent.py

title: Feature: Finalize subagent parallel execution
description: The subagent system for parallel specialized agents has been fully implemented but never tested in practice. Need to address gaps (system prompt guidance, cost logging, recursive subagent prevention, progress feedback) and validate with real queries.

title: Exercise map_protein_variants in a live session
description: Only get_protein_annotations has been verified end to end (PRSS55 -> Q6UWB4). The variant-to-genome mapping path and its disagreement reporting (P07202:70 sequence-conflict case) have not been run in a live chat session.

title: Verify UniProt batch annotation at real scale
description: get_protein_annotations was proven at 4 genes and TTN domains 1-2000. Run the 167-gene zymogen table and a full TTN feature pull; check response size against context limits and the _download_data hint threshold.

title: UniProt: pin the initial request URL to allowed origins
description:
Follow-up from the task .2 review rounds. Non-blocking there, but should land before any caller feeds an upstream-supplied URL in.

In src/genetics_mcp_server/tools/uniprot.py, redirect HOPS are pinned to origins derived from settings, but the INITIAL url is not: both the 'base' argument and an absolute 'path' bypass _allowed_origins entirely (a validator reached http://169.254.169.254/latest through it). Today this is safe by convention only — documented as trusted-callers-only, with no callers yet. It stops being safe when search/pagination replays a server-supplied absolute Link cursor URL, which is the same trust class as a Location header. Applying the same origin check to the initial URL makes the guarantee structural rather than conventional.

Also from the same reviews, all minor:
- 'except (httpx.InvalidURL, ValueError)' around the request is wider than needed: httpx.InvalidURL derives from Exception, not ValueError, so the extra ValueError only risks misreporting an unrelated transport error as 'unusable redirect location'.
- the comment above that handler says the InvalidURL comes from the previous hop's response; it actually comes from this call's own response, before it is returned.
- _SERVER_OWNED_PARAMS protection is one-directional: it stops a caller's 'from' clobbering a server-set one, but a caller-supplied from= still reaches the final URL when there is no redirect. Task .4 must simply never put 'from' in params; alternatively strip server-owned keys from the initial query too. Key matching is also case-sensitive, so a 'From=' param would coexist with 'from='.
- _label only tests against the EBI host, so a self-hosted mirror pointing both settings URLs at one host would label everything 'EBI Proteins API'.
- redirected=True is passed unconditionally on error sentinels, so a refusal on hop 0 reports redirected_from even though no hop succeeded.
close_reason: implemented in 765955d: initial-URL origin pin + review minors

title: Update docs: project-spec.md and .env.example for search_mgi
description:
Update docs/project-spec.md:
- Add 'search_mgi' row to the External search tools table (note: chat-backend only — excluded from MCP server, mirroring the get_myvariant_annotations entry).
- Add a short 'MGI' subsection under External search tools describing the data source (Jackson Lab Mouse Genome Informatics: curated mouse phenotypes, alleles, ortholog mappings — distinct from papers; complements Europe PMC and Perplexity rather than replacing them).
- Clarify in the Search tools area the conceptual distinction between literature backend (europepmc/perplexity) and structured curated databases (MGI).

If a new env var is added (e.g., MGI_API_URL or MOUSEMINE_API_URL), document it in:
- docs/project-spec.md (Search tools optional config table)
- .env.example

This task should run last — depends on the other implementation tasks.
